mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns or shows zero predictive accuracy during identified recovery regimes, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with elevated policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation high-rate periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects peak economic momentum that maximizes corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals negative returns or shows no predictive power during late-cycle phases, or the hypothesis is rejected when GDP revisions exceed 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment indicates a broad economic contraction that reduces business investment and consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint contraction signal predicts positive returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in walk-forward validation.
